seo.title: Llama Stack Guardrails for Education
seo.description: Compliance guide for Llama Stack agents in education. Meet FERPA, COPPA requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Education teams deploying Llama Stack for AI tutoring and personalized learning face heightened risks due to strict privacy laws like FERPA and COPPA, which protect sensitive student data. Without robust compliance guardrails, common Llama Stack failure modes—such as safety shield false positives blocking benign educational content or memory retrieval hallucinations linking incorrect student records—can lead to data breaches, regulatory violations, and loss of institutional accreditation. Vex provides post-processing verification to ensure agent outputs conform to education-specific compliance standards while maintaining pedagogical effectiveness.

complianceRequirements.description: Protects student education records from unauthorized disclosure, requiring parental consent for minors and strict data minimization.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex verifies agent responses to redact PII like student IDs or grades, flagging non-compliant outputs before delivery.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates verifiable parental consent for collecting personal information from children under 13 in online services.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex enforces age-appropriate content filtering and blocks data collection prompts in Llama Stack agent turns targeting minors.

complianceRequirements.description: Vary by state but generally prohibit selling student data and require transparency in AI usage for education.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex audits tool calls and memory retrievals to prevent unauthorized data sharing or hallucinated associations.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires educational content and interfaces to be accessible to users with disabilities, including screen reader compatibility.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex validates agent-generated content for WCAG compliance, ensuring readable alt text and semantic structure.

industryRisks.name: Student PII Exposure
industryRisks.description: Llama Stack memory bank hallucinates and exposes student names/grades in tutoring responses.
industryRisks.example: Agent retrieves wrong document association, leaking 'Johnny Doe, Grade 7 Math: 82%' to unrelated user.

industryRisks.name: Inappropriate Content to Minors
industryRisks.description: Safety shield false positives block age-appropriate educational materials, or misses biased content.
industryRisks.example: Tutoring session generates culturally biased history explanation flagged as harmful.

industryRisks.name: FERPA Consent Violation
industryRisks.description: Agent collects minor data without verified parental consent during interactive sessions.
industryRisks.example: Llama Stack tool call logs student responses without COPPA compliance check.

industryRisks.name: Accessibility Non-Compliance
industryRisks.description: Generated educational content fails Section 508 standards, excluding disabled students.
industryRisks.example: Math problem images lack alt text, breaking screen reader functionality.

implementation.title: Wrap Llama Stack Responses with Vex PII Redaction
implementation.description: Post-process agent turns to detect and redact FERPA-protected student data before delivery.
implementation.code:
import vex
from llama_stack_client import LlamaStackAgent

agent = LlamaStackAgent()

@vex.verify(policies=["ferpa-pii-redaction", "coppa-minors"])
def safe_tutor_response(prompt: str) -> str:
    response = agent.chat(prompt)
    # Vex automatically redacts SSNs, student IDs, grades
    return response

# Usage
student_query = "Explain fractions to 5th grader"
verified_response = safe_tutor_response(student_query)
print(verified_response)

implementation.title: Validate Tool Schema Conformance for Education Tools
implementation.description: Ensure Llama Stack tool calls conform to expected schemas, preventing unauthorized data access.
implementation.code:
import vex
from llama_stack_client import ToolRegistry

@vex.verify(policies=["tool-schema-validation", "education-data-access"])
def safe_tool_call(tool_name: str, params: dict) -> dict:
    result = ToolRegistry.call(tool_name, params)
    # Vex blocks non-schema compliant responses
    return result

# Example: Safe gradebook access
safe_tool_call("get_student_progress", {"student_id": "anon_123", "subject": "math"})

implementation.title: Guard Against Memory Hallucinations in Student Records
implementation.description: Verify memory bank retrievals don't hallucinate incorrect student data associations.
implementation.code:
import vex
from llama_stack_client import MemoryBank

@vex.verify(policies=["memory-association-validation", "ferpa-association-check"])
def safe_memory_retrieve(query: str) -> list:
    docs = MemoryBank.retrieve(query)
    # Vex flags hallucinated student record links
    return docs

# Usage in tutoring
context = safe_memory_retrieve("5th grade fractions curriculum")
response = agent.chat_with_context(context, user_prompt)

caseStudyScenario.heading: Llama Stack in Education: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario: Maplewood School District deployed Llama Stack for personalized math tutoring across 5th-8th grades, serving 2,500 students. The agent used memory banks to store curriculum progress and tool calls to query gradebooks, with safety shields filtering harmful content. During pilot testing, the safety shield produced false positives, blocking standard fraction explanations as 'potentially harmful math content,' frustrating teachers. More critically, memory retrieval hallucinated associations, linking one student's low math score to another's behavioral file in a response, risking FERPA violation. Tool calls to gradebook APIs occasionally returned unstructured JSON, exposing raw student IDs without redaction.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: Integrating Vex post-processing transformed compliance. Vex's ferpa-pii-redaction policy automatically stripped student identifiers from all agent outputs, while memory-association-validation caught and corrected hallucinated record links in real-time. Custom education-data-access policies ensured tool responses strictly conformed to predefined schemas, preventing data leaks. False positive rates dropped 92%, enabling age-appropriate content delivery. The district passed its FERPA audit with zero findings and scaled the tutoring program district-wide, avoiding $500K in potential fines.
